description: Fix workplan execution to scaffold NEW files before agents edit them — the fundamental flaw that broke managed-agents-autonomy workplan

tasks.name: Add scaffold_file() function
tasks.description: Add scaffold_file() function to workplan_executor.rs that creates empty files with proper module structure for Rust, TypeScript, and other file types
tasks.done_command: cargo check -p hex-nexus 2>/dev/null && echo PASS || echo FAIL

phases.name: Add file existence check in execute_phase()

tasks.name: File existence check before agent spawn
tasks.description: Modify execute_phase() to check if task.files exist before spawning agents. If not, scaffold them first.
tasks.done_command: cargo check -p hex-nexus 2>/dev/null && echo PASS || echo FAIL

tasks.name: Classify tasks as NEW or MODIFY
tasks.description: Add task classification: if file exists in any crate's Cargo.toml/paths, it's MODIFY; otherwise it's NEW (needs scaffolding)
tasks.done_command: cargo check -p hex-core 2>/dev/null && echo PASS || echo FAIL

phases.name: Prompt enrichment with scaffolded file list

tasks.name: Update agent prompt with scaffolded file info
tasks.description: After scaffolding, update the agent prompt to indicate which files were scaffolded so the agent knows to populate them
tasks.done_command: cargo check -p hex-nexus 2>/dev/null && echo PASS || echo FAIL

tasks.name: Run managed-agents workplan again
tasks.description: Execute the managed-agents-autonomy workplan again and verify all 18 tasks complete without file-not-found errors
tasks.done_command: echo 'Run workplan and verify P0 tasks complete'

successCriteria: Execute phase checks file existence before spawning agents
NEW files are scaffolded before agents edit them
All 18 tasks in managed-agents workplan pass
